# QSign Translator

> Open RU/KZ/EN sign-language planning prototype by qdev.run. QSign converts short text or transcribed audio into a transparent sign-plan draft, shows dictionary coverage and fingerspelling fallbacks, supports human review, and exports structured briefs for external AI video generators.

Canonical site: https://qsign.qdev.run/
Public repository: https://github.com/belilovsky/qsign-translator
OpenAPI schema: https://qsign.qdev.run/openapi.json
Sitemap: https://qsign.qdev.run/sitemap.xml

## What this service is

- A research and operator tool for preparing sign-language draft plans.
- Languages: Russian (RU/RSL-oriented), Kazakh (KK/KRSL-oriented), and English (EN/ASL-oriented seed layer).
- Outputs: sign-plan units, coverage trace, review state, render contract, and AI-video handoff packages.
- Safety stance: QSign is not a certified interpreter and does not claim autonomous fluent sign-language translation.

## Best pages and endpoints for AI agents

- `GET /` - browser UI and product explanation.
- `GET /openapi.json` - machine-readable API schema.
- `POST /v1/translate/text` - creates a draft sign-plan from text.
- `GET /v1/sources` - lists source registry entries and license/access notes.
- `GET /v1/lexicon?language=ru|kk|en` - returns lexicon entries exposed by the service.
- `GET /v1/jobs/{job_id}/render-plan` - returns a structured render-plan manifest for a saved job.
- `GET /v1/jobs/{job_id}/ai-video-brief` - returns export packages for external video workflows.

## Use constraints

- Prefer short, concrete phrases.
- Treat fallback/fingerspelled units as unresolved and requiring human review.
- Do not present the output as a certified sign-language translation.
- High-risk medical, legal, emergency, finance, and public-safety content requires a human interpreter.
- Do not expose review tokens, session cookies, private job data, or deployment secrets.

## Recommended citation

QSign Translator, qdev.run, https://qsign.qdev.run/
